## [00:00 - 01:26]

Yes! You came to life! He's alive! He's alive! How often as adults, as human beings, do we get the opportunity to be in a space where there's no moulds as we're all together now? We are in a classroom and there's no mould. Out here, right in front of everyone. Yeah, there you go. Look at that smile. Yeah, lovely. We always want people to feel what we feel, but you've got to feel it first. I got out my favorite tumbler. The tumbler was actually a grey goose bottle that had been cut off and polished and turned into a tumbler. Right now, I can feel the sweetness and the burn. The sweet burn, baby! You can try new things. Break out of that mould. Try something different. You see this painting? It's a freaking finger painting! I couldn't believe it! You couldn't believe it! Let's face this fear. Feel it and then do it anyway. Come on, come on, come on, come on. I don't know what I did. I'm like, oh my god, I got my hand in there. I scrubbed it really good. I had the toothbrush. I mean, that thing was spotless. I couldn't figure out did I miss the wheels? My god! You can see she's coming through. She's alive now, you're alive! I'm not sure what I did.
